title: ErrorBoundary
description: Catches errors thrown in child components and renders a fallback.
signature: ErrorBoundary(props: { fallback: MaybeAccessor<CReactNode> | ((err: any, reset: () => void) => CReactNode); children: MaybeAccessor<CReactNode> }): JSXElement
prop_fallback_desc: Static fallback or render function called with the caught error and a reset function.
prop_children_desc: Content to render. Errors in this subtree are caught.
usage_guarding_the_sweep: The uptime monitor wraps its whole fleet of <0>HttpCheck</0> probes in one <1>ErrorBoundary</1>. A throw from any probe is caught and passed to the <2>fallback</2> render function instead of crashing the sweep.
usage_rendering_the_fault: The fallback receives the caught <0>error</0> and renders <1>MonitorFault</1>, which logs that checks have halted. The rest of the tree keeps running.
in_the_wild: See this boundary keep a status page alive in the <0>uptime-monitor walkthrough</0>.
